ancient-fruit.intent: Best long-term greenhouse crop and endgame wine engine.
ancient-fruit.bestChoice: Keg into Wine with Artisan, then age only when you have spare Cask space.
ancient-fruit.doThis: Fill the Greenhouse or Ginger Island with Ancient Fruit first, then scale Kegs until every weekly harvest can be processed.
ancient-fruit.avoidThis: Do not sell raw Ancient Fruit once you have Keg capacity. Raw selling is mostly a temporary cash-flow move.

starfruit.intent: Highest-value repeat crop for Oasis, greenhouse, and wine production.
starfruit.bestChoice: Keg into Wine with Artisan. Use Casks on Starfruit Wine when you want maximum per-bottle value.
starfruit.doThis: Buy seeds from the Oasis, grow in Summer or protected spaces, and reserve Keg capacity before harvest day.
starfruit.avoidThis: Do not compare Starfruit only by raw sell price. The real value is the 2,250g Wine base value and 3,150g Artisan value.

pumpkin.intent: Best Fall single-harvest crop, useful for bundles, gifts, and high-value Juice.
pumpkin.bestChoice: Keg into Juice when Kegs are available; use 3x3 planting if you want Giant Crop chances.
pumpkin.doThis: Plant on Fall 1 for two normal harvests, or use Speed-Gro to create more flexible timing.
pumpkin.avoidThis: Do not ignore bundle and gift uses. Keep enough Pumpkins before processing the whole harvest.

strawberry.intent: Best Spring regrowth crop after the Egg Festival and a strong Year 2 setup crop.
strawberry.bestChoice: Grow for repeated harvests; Keg excess berries when Keg capacity is not needed by higher-value fruit.
strawberry.doThis: Buy heavily at the Egg Festival, plant immediately, and save some fruit for Seed Makers before Year 2.
strawberry.avoidThis: Do not spend all money without leaving enough for Summer seeds. Strawberry is good, but cash flow matters.

hops.intent: Daily Summer harvest crop for Pale Ale and high Keg throughput.
hops.bestChoice: Keg into Pale Ale if you have enough Kegs to keep up with daily harvests.
hops.doThis: Plan trellis pathing and Keg count before planting large Hops fields.
hops.avoidThis: Do not mass plant Hops without Kegs. Raw Hops are low value and daily harvesting becomes tedious.

truffle.intent: Pig product for daily outdoor animal profit and Truffle Oil production.
truffle.bestChoice: Process into Truffle Oil with Artisan unless your profession setup strongly favors raw iridium Truffles.
truffle.doThis: Prioritize Deluxe Barns, happy pigs, and enough Oil Makers before scaling pig count.
truffle.avoidThis: Do not judge pigs by winter output. Pigs do not find Truffles in winter or rain.

caviar.intent: Premium Fish Pond product made from Sturgeon Roe.
caviar.bestChoice: Use Sturgeon Fish Ponds and Preserves Jars to turn Roe into Caviar.
caviar.doThis: Build at least one Sturgeon pond early if you want steady passive income and Missing Bundle coverage.
caviar.avoidThis: Do not use random low-value Roe as a substitute when the goal is Caviar. Only Sturgeon Roe makes Caviar.

fairy-honey.intent: High-value Fall Bee House product from Fairy Rose flowers.
fairy-honey.bestChoice: Sell raw Fairy Rose Honey. Do not turn it into Mead.
fairy-honey.doThis: Place Bee Houses around Fairy Roses and keep the flowers alive through Fall production cycles.
fairy-honey.avoidThis: Do not put Fairy Honey in a Keg. Mead destroys most of the premium flower value.

diamond.intent: Best general Crystalarium item and strong universal gift.
diamond.bestChoice: Put Diamonds in Crystalariums for stable passive gold unless you need Jade for Staircases.
diamond.doThis: Use your first Diamonds for Museum and gifting, then standardize Crystalariums around Diamond or Jade depending on your goal.
diamond.avoidThis: Do not sell early Diamonds before considering Crystalarium, gifts, and quests.

oak-resin.intent: Core machine crafting material for Keg scaling.
oak-resin.bestChoice: Save for Kegs until your wine and Pale Ale operation is fully built.
oak-resin.doThis: Tap Oak Trees early and keep a dedicated Oak Resin supply line running.
oak-resin.avoidThis: Do not sell Oak Resin in early or mid game. The lost Keg capacity costs far more than the sell price.
